summary: CI/tooling and config cleanup to reduce `ninja diff` false positives. The PR changed the build workflow so the link job runs `ninja` with errors temporarily ignored and then always runs `ninja diff`, and it corrected GALE01 split/symbol metadata for several small data/rodata/sdata/sdata2 objects whose previous sizes/scopes overreached into padding or neighboring objects. Two item source tweaks supported the metadata: `itheiho.c` internal helper prototypes were made `static`, and `itsword.c` replaced a dummy stack array with `PAD_STACK(8)`.
what_changed: In `.github/workflows/build.yml`, the `link` job changed from `ninja || ninja diff` to `set +e; ninja; set -e; ninja diff`, so the canonical diff target runs after the build attempt instead of only as a shell OR fallback.

In `config/GALE01/splits.txt`, many section end addresses were tightened to non-rounded object ends, including examples such as `plstale.c` `.sbss` ending at `0x804D647A` instead of `0x804D6480`, `itsword.c` `.sdata2` ending at `0x804DC8FC` instead of `0x804DC900`, `itheiho.c` `.data` ending at `0x803F8465` instead of `0x803F8468`, and item `.sdata` ranges ending at `...2D`, `...4D`, or `...65` instead of the next aligned boundary.

In `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, several anonymous or local constants/strings were split out from larger global-looking ranges, e.g. `@234` at `0x803F6718`, `@238` at `0x803F8440`, `@264` at `0x803F9428`, and multiple `.sdata` string labels such as `@216`, `@217`, `@236`, and `@237`.

Symbol metadata was corrected for size/type/scope: examples include `it_803B86C8` changing from size `0x10` 4-byte data to size `0xC` float data, `Chan` shrinking from size `0x8` to `0x4`, and `staleAttackInstance` replacing `lbl_804D6478` as a size `0x2` `.sbss` object.
In `src/melee/it/items/itheiho.c`, 22 forward declarations for internal helper functions were changed from external declarations to `static`, aligning source linkage with local symbol metadata.
In `src/melee/it/items/itsword.c`, an explicit dummy `u8 _[8] = { 0 };` stack pad was replaced with `PAD_STACK(8)` after local variable declarations.
smart_moves: The workflow fix separates the build attempt from the diff check: temporarily disabling `set -e` around `ninja` lets CI proceed to `ninja diff`, then reenabling `set -e` ensures the actual diff result controls failure.

The config changes treat small string/float constants as exact-sized local objects rather than rounded global placeholders, avoiding alignment padding being interpreted as meaningful section contents.
Making `itheiho.c` helper declarations `static` is a low-risk source change that makes object symbol visibility agree with the revised `symbols.txt` local scopes.
Using `PAD_STACK(8)` in `itsword.c` records stack-padding intent directly and avoids a misleading dummy variable while preserving the matching tactic.
decomp_lessons: False positives in diff tooling can come from metadata, not source code: over-broad split ranges and inaccurate symbol sizes/scopes can make padding or adjacent literals appear as mismatches.
For GALE01 split maintenance, section end addresses should reflect the true end of the object data, not necessarily the next aligned address.
When symbol analysis reveals local compiler-generated constants or string literals, prefer anonymous/local `@NNN` labels with accurate size/type metadata over broad `it_` global placeholders.
Source linkage should match symbol visibility. If functions are file-local, mark prototypes `static` so object symbols and config scopes stay consistent.
For intentional stack padding, prefer project macros like `PAD_STACK(n)` over dummy local arrays when available; this improves readability while preserving decomp matching behavior.
CI jobs that are meant to report binary diffs should run the diff target explicitly after a build attempt rather than relying on shell short-circuit behavior.
naming_conventions: Anonymous local compiler constants and string literals are represented as `@NNN` labels in `symbols.txt` when no semantic name is known.
Address-derived names such as `it_803F83F0` remain for global or still-unidentified data objects.
Known semantic globals should replace generic labels when evidence exists, as shown by `lbl_804D6478` becoming `staleAttackInstance`.
File-local functions in C should use `static` declarations when their symbols are local.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Tighten split ranges to exact data ends to prevent padding bytes from participating in diff comparisons.
Correct symbol type metadata, e.g. distinguish `data:float`, `data:double`, `data:string`, and `data:4byte`, so generated/linker metadata matches the actual binary layout.
Use `PAD_STACK(8)` instead of an initialized dummy byte array for explicit stack layout preservation.
Use local `@NNN` symbol entries for small constants or strings embedded between larger data objects.
Mark internal helper prototypes `static` to match local symbol linkage and avoid exporting unintended symbols.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that changed `build.yml` `ninja diff` behavior or CI diff reporting.
Find PRs that convert dummy stack arrays to `PAD_STACK` in item files.
Find PRs that tighten `config/GALE01/splits.txt` section ends to exact unaligned addresses.
Find PRs that convert address-derived `it_` symbols to local `@NNN` string or float constants.
Find PRs that make item helper function declarations `static` to match local symbol scope.
